title: Evangelion 222 Australian Packaging
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.22 (Australian Packaging)             Quote Post by insomniac » Mon Jun 13, 2011 10:09 pm Just receive this in the mail. A while ago, there was a thread on the difference between the menu design of 1.11 across the globe. So I thought I'd share how the Australian packaging of 2.22 is like. Slipcase printed on fairly cheap cardboard. No gloss, matte lamination, nor texture found in the Japanese/Hong Kong editions. [URL=http://img822.imageshack.us/i/eva1e.jpg/] [/URL] [URL=http://img861.imageshack.us/i/eva2f.jpg/] [/URL] The design of the back is more or less the same as the US version. [URL=http://img137.imageshack.us/i/eva3u.jpg/] [/URL] At least the disc looks good, pretty much the same as the original except it's in orange instead of black. Comes with a booklet much like 1.11 [URL=http://img195.imageshack.us/i/eva4q.jpg/] [/URL] Instead of providing us with an alternate cover like in 1.11, we get one of the promo posters on the inner insert. [URL=http://img710.imageshack.us/i/eva5h.jpg/] [/URL] Some pictures in the booklet. [URL=http://img600.imageshack.us/i/eva6i.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jun 13, 2011 10:36 pm Very nice.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Jun 13, 2011 10:48 pm Very nice indeed. I'm actually kinda jealous... EDIT: Lol at the tagline! "You Can (Not) Miss the Evolution of Evangelion!"

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Jun 13, 2011 10:53 pm Their version of the PG rating is interesting in that it doesn't mention the nudity. Not that I think it should, but the MPAA would definitely note it, no matter how trivial.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Jun 13, 2011 11:29 pm So in Australia you can drop an 'F' Bomb and still have a PG rating?

[]:             Quote Post by insomniac » Tue Jun 14, 2011 12:19 am Warren Peace wrote: Their version of the PG rating is interesting in that it doesn't mention the nudity. Not that I think it should, but the MPAA would definitely note it, no matter how trivial.

[]: Evangelion 2.22 AUSTRALIAN VERSION w/ extras!             Quote Post by KaaVI » Tue Jun 14, 2011 2:59 am I just got my copy today, It comes in a slip case and a miniature artbook with lots of facts and story board concepts! EVEN A COMMENTARY OPTION ON THE DVD!!!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Jun 14, 2011 9:23 pm Looks good KaaVI.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 14, 2011 9:26 pm Looks mostly the same as Funimation's U.S. release (in terms of the extras, the packaging was slightly different). Good stuff though.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Jun 14, 2011 10:08 pm It's similar enough that I wonder why they went to the trouble of changing anything at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:10 pm So, it looks like the packaging for 2.22 is the same all over the world then, huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:31 pm Did the US box come in a regular plastic case with Eva-02 on the cover with an Orange slipcase? I haven't bought the Funi release yet, but I thought it was just a fold out box and not something that had a picture on it.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Jun 15, 2011 3:44 am @Shogo-Kun: IIRC 2.xx German's release has unique cover art. There was a thread made about it late last year. @Ornette: I recall the Funi release has the same orange slip cover, but with Mari on the case cover. Someone will have to dig that up. Also, Madman has updated their Evangelion Site with 2.22 information.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Jun 15, 2011 6:01 am EvangelionFan wrote: I recall the Funi release has the same orange slip cover, but with Mari on the case cover.

[]:             Quote Post by Cesario » Wed Jun 15, 2011 8:38 am i picked up my 2.22 today went to JB and had to wait an hour cause they hadnt checked it into the system yet :P so me and my mate waited, got it, watched it, watched it with commentary, loved it deff worth the wait is there any info any where on whats included in the diff versions (2.0 / 222 / funi / madman) or is it all the same?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jun 15, 2011 10:25 am >Ever since Eva-02 fought the 6th Angel Gaghiel at Sea. Nice fact-checking there for 2.0, Madman. (It even says "NGE" in various spots on the website!) Anyway, here's what Funimation's 2.22 DVD and Blu-Ray had (and Funi's 1.11): http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=10991 This post discusses the 2.22 Funimation DVD/BD extras.

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Wed Jun 15, 2011 12:58 pm "Mild animated violence"... With Eva 03's intestines flying across the screen? Wow. I'd hate to see what kind of movies get a higher rating than PG in Australia.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Jun 15, 2011 1:20 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: >Ever since Eva-02 fought the 6th Angel Gaghiel at Sea. Nice fact-checking there for 2.0, Madman. (It even says "NGE" in various spots on the website!)

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Wed Jun 15, 2011 7:31 pm Aozora wrote: "Mild animated violence"... With Eva 03's intestines flying across the screen? Wow. I'd hate to see what kind of movies get a higher rating than PG in Australia.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Jun 15, 2011 7:48 pm Everyone knows cartoon are for kids...right?
